narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after all the effort you've put into your law school dreams, and I want to walk you through why our prediction model saw a 40.9% chance of you not passing the bar exam, leading to this tough call on your application. Picture your profile coming together like chapters in a story: the strongest thread pulling toward that risk was your Year 3 decile of 4, which stood out as a middling performance in a competitive field, signaling to the model that sustaining top momentum might be challenging. Right behind that was your Year 1 decile of 1, an even tougher start that painted an early picture of hurdles in keeping up with rigorous coursework. Your upper-middle family income, while a solid foundation, unfortunately nudged the outlook lower in this context, perhaps reflecting patterns where that bracket doesn't always translate to the extra academic edge needed here. Then there's your LSAT score of 25, which the model saw as another pull toward struggle on high-stakes tests like the bar. Your Hispanic background and undergraduate GPA of 2.4 added to that cautious view, weaving in historical trends the model has learned from. Even your part-time status and male gender factored in, with the former tipping toward risk and the latter offering a slight counterbalance toward success. It's this combination—like a mosaic of early stumbles, mid-tier finishes, and those key test scores—that overall sketched a riskier path forward, even if each piece alone isn't the full story. You're clearly driven, with real potential shining through, and this isn't a judgment on your worth—it's just the model's honest read based on patterns from thousands like you. Many have turned similar profiles around with targeted prep, so consider bolstering those early grades or LSAT prep as your next chapter. You've got this resilience; keep pushing.
